description: Missing OFCs for ADA conditions using project-local existing VOFC data

vulnerabilities.vulnerability_text: Electric service may rely on a single feed or limited diversity.
Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for EP_SHARED_ENTRY: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for EP_COLOCATED_CORRIDOR: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for EP_NO_PRIORITY_RESTORATION: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
Backup power capability may be absent or insufficient for critical operations.
Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for EP_NO_FUEL_CONTINUITY: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
Exterior electric components are exposed to vehicle impact without protective measures, increasing the likelihood of damage that disrupts power service.
The protection posture for exterior electric components exposed to vehicle impact is unknown, limiting confidence in their ability to withstand a strike.
Communications paths may share facility entry points with other utilities.
Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for COM_COLOCATED_CORRIDOR: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
Exterior communications components are exposed to vehicle impact and lack protective measures, increasing the chance of a disruptive strike.
The level of vehicle impact protection for exterior communications components is unknown, leaving potential exposure unaddressed.
Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for COM_NO_PRIORITY_RESTORATION: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for COM_NO_CONTINGENCY_PLAN: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
IT services may rely on a single carrier or path with no redundant options.
Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for IT_SHARED_ENTRY: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
Exterior IT infrastructure (e.g., network cabinets, conduit) is exposed to vehicle impact without protective measures, risking service disruption.
Vehicle impact protection for exterior IT infrastructure is unknown, creating uncertainty in resilience planning.
Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for IT_NO_PRIORITY_RESTORATION: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
The facility relies on a single water service connection, creating a potential single point of failure. A disruption could degrade operations.
Water service connections enter the site at the same geographic location, creating a potential single point of failure.
Water service components are collocated with other utilities, increasing the likelihood a single incident disrupts multiple services.
The facility has not identified its water provider and relevant upstream dependency information, which may limit effective coordination during disruptions.
The facility does not participate in a priority or coordinated restoration plan with the water provider, which may delay restoration during high-impact events.
The facility does not have a documented contingency/coordination plan with the water provider for extended disruption.
The facility does not have an alternate/backup water source to use if primary water service is disrupted.
The facility has an alternate water source, but it cannot support core operational needs during an extended disruption.
The alternate water source depends on commercial power or another external service, which may limit its availability during outages.
Water-based fire suppression depends on water service, and no secondary supply approach has been identified if water pressure/supply is lost.
The facility has not evaluated operational impacts if fire suppression water is unavailable during prolonged disruption.
Onsite water pumping/boosting depends on commercial power without backup power, increasing disruption risk during outages.
Onsite water pumping/boosting cannot be manually overridden if automation fails, which may increase the likelihood of service interruption.
The facility lacks a method to detect/alert on pump failure, low pressure, or storage depletion, which may delay response and increase impacts.
The facility relies on a single source for critical onsite water pump/control components, which may extend downtime during failures.
The facility relies on a single wastewater service connection, creating a potential single point of failure. A disruption could degrade operations.
Wastewater service connections enter/leave the site at the same geographic location, creating a potential single point of failure.
Wastewater service components are collocated with other utilities, increasing the likelihood a single incident disrupts multiple services.
The facility has not identified its wastewater provider and/or primary upstream dependencies supporting service, which may limit effective coordination during disruptions.
The facility does not participate in a priority or coordinated restoration plan with the wastewater provider, which may delay restoration during high-impact events.
The facility does not have a documented contingency/coordination plan with the wastewater provider for extended disruption.
Onsite wastewater pumping relies on commercial power without backup power, increasing the likelihood of overflow/backflow or operational disruption during outages.
Onsite wastewater pumping cannot be manually overridden if automation fails, which may increase the likelihood of service interruption or overflow.
The facility lacks a method to detect/alert on pump failure, high level, or backflow risk, which may delay response and increase impacts.
The facility relies on a single source for critical onsite wastewater pump components, which may extend downtime during failures.
The facility lacks temporary holding/containment capability to prevent overflow/backflow during wastewater service disruption, which may force shutdown.
The facility has not evaluated regulatory/operational constraints that could force shutdown during prolonged wastewater disruption.

injections.vulnerability_text: Electric service may rely on a single feed or limited diversity.
library_ofcs: Evaluate the possibility of establishing a microgrid or on-site generation capability that can operate independently and provide backup power during grid failures.

injections.vulnerability_text: Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for EP_SHARED_ENTRY: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
library_ofcs: Evaluate the feasibility of establishing additional electric service connections with geographic separation.

injections.vulnerability_text: Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for EP_COLOCATED_CORRIDOR: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
library_ofcs: Evaluate the feasibility of establishing additional electric service connections with geographic separation to address co-located entry points.

injections.vulnerability_text: Backup power capability may be absent or insufficient for critical operations.
library_ofcs: Document detailed backup-power operating procedures to ensure operators can sustain critical functions during extended grid loss.
Formalize fuel sustainment and resupply coordination plans for multi-day outages.

injections.vulnerability_text: Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for EP_NO_FUEL_CONTINUITY: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
library_ofcs: Conduct regular testing and maintenance of backup fuel systems to ensure reliability during extended outages.

injections.vulnerability_text: Exterior electric components are exposed to vehicle impact without protective measures, increasing the likelihood of damage that disrupts power service.
library_ofcs: Evaluate the installation of bollards or other physical barriers around critical exterior electrical equipment to mitigate vehicle impact risks.
Implement a standoff distance between the facility’s perimeter and critical exterior electrical assets to reduce the risk of vehicle impacts.
Reroute or relocate critical exterior electrical equipment away from high-traffic areas to reduce the risk of vehicle impacts.

injections.vulnerability_text: The protection posture for exterior electric components exposed to vehicle impact is unknown, limiting confidence in their ability to withstand a strike.
library_ofcs: Consider implementing bollards and barriers around exterior electrical components to mitigate vehicle impact risk.
Evaluate the feasibility of installing reinforced concrete barriers or other robust physical protection around critical exterior electrical assets.
Install standoff distances and grade separations to prevent vehicles from reaching critical electrical infrastructure.

injections.vulnerability_text: Communications paths may share facility entry points with other utilities.
library_ofcs: Implement physical barriers to separate entry points used by different utilities, reducing the risk of cascading failures.
Coordinate with utility providers to establish priority restoration agreements for shared entry points.

injections.vulnerability_text: Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for COM_COLOCATED_CORRIDOR: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
library_ofcs: Evaluate and implement diversified communications architecture using distinct carriers or transport paths where feasible for critical voice coordination functions.
Document voice coordination methods, carrier contacts, and escalation procedures to ensure clear restoration roles and coordination.
Establish restoration roles and coordination procedures for critical communications dependencies to ensure timely recovery.
Consider using Telecommunications Service Priority (TSP) to expedite restoration of critical voice communication services.

injections.vulnerability_text: The level of vehicle impact protection for exterior communications components is unknown, leaving potential exposure unaddressed.
library_ofcs: Install bollards or barriers around exterior communications components exposed to vehicle impact.
Increase standoff distance between exterior communications components and potential impact zones.

injections.vulnerability_text: Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for COM_NO_PRIORITY_RESTORATION: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
library_ofcs: Develop a contingency plan for voice communications that includes alternative pathways or backup systems to maintain operational continuity during disruptions.
Maintain a detailed record of current carrier contacts and escalation procedures for rapid reporting and restoration coordination during outages.

injections.vulnerability_text: Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for COM_NO_CONTINGENCY_PLAN: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
library_ofcs: Establish a formal contingency plan with the telecommunications service provider to ensure rapid restoration of voice coordination services.
Develop a secondary communication pathway for critical voice coordination to ensure redundancy in case of primary pathway failure.

injections.vulnerability_text: IT services may rely on a single carrier or path with no redundant options.
library_ofcs: Consider implementing a secondary data transport provider for critical services to ensure redundancy.

injections.vulnerability_text: Infrastructure dependency vulnerability for IT_SHARED_ENTRY: loss or degradation of dependency may disrupt operations without adequate resilience controls.
library_ofcs: Consider establishing a contingency plan with IT service providers for rapid restoration in case of disruptions.

injections.vulnerability_text: Exterior IT infrastructure (e.g., network cabinets, conduit) is exposed to vehicle impact without protective measures, risking service disruption.
library_ofcs: Install bollards or barriers around exposed IT infrastructure to prevent vehicle impact.
Construct reinforced barriers or concrete walls around exterior IT infrastructure to provide additional protection against vehicle impact.

injections.vulnerability_text: The facility relies on a single water service connection, creating a potential single point of failure. A disruption could degrade operations.
library_ofcs: Consider installing a secondary well or onsite storage tanks to serve as an independent backup water source during extended disruptions.
Consider developing a detailed contingency plan with the local water utility provider to ensure priority restoration of critical services during disruptions.

injections.vulnerability_text: Water service components are collocated with other utilities, increasing the likelihood a single incident disrupts multiple services.
library_ofcs: Consider implementing physical separation or diversification of water service entry points to reduce the risk of simultaneous disruption.
Explore options for relocating critical water service components to separate utility corridors, reducing shared vulnerability with other services.
Develop a plan for emergency rerouting or temporary connections if primary water service is disrupted due to shared entry vulnerability.
Coordinate with local utilities and emergency services to ensure they understand the critical nature of separate utility corridors for water service.

injections.vulnerability_text: The facility does not participate in a priority or coordinated restoration plan with the water provider, which may delay restoration during high-impact events.
library_ofcs: Consider establishing a formal agreement with the water utility to ensure priority restoration in case of service disruptions.
Develop a detailed emergency response plan that includes specific steps for contacting the water provider and initiating restoration procedures.

injections.vulnerability_text: The facility does not have a documented contingency/coordination plan with the water provider for extended disruption.
library_ofcs: Consider developing a detailed contingency plan with specific steps and timelines for communication, resource allocation, and operational adjustments during extended disruptions.

injections.vulnerability_text: The facility has an alternate water source, but it cannot support core operational needs during an extended disruption.
library_ofcs: Consider enhancing the capacity and duration of the alternate water source to better support core operational needs.
Evaluate the feasibility of establishing a secondary water supply approach, such as an additional well or connection, to supplement the existing alternate source.
Consider implementing a system to monitor and manage the usage of the alternate water source in real-time, ensuring efficient allocation during disruptions.

injections.vulnerability_text: Water-based fire suppression depends on water service, and no secondary supply approach has been identified if water pressure/supply is lost.
library_ofcs: Consider installing an onsite fire water storage tank to provide a secondary supply for fire suppression during primary water disruptions.
Evaluate the feasibility of establishing an agreement with a neighboring facility or utility to provide emergency water supply for fire suppression.
Develop and document operational procedures to manage fire suppression during prolonged water disruptions, including manual operation of fire suppression systems if automated controls fail.

injections.vulnerability_text: The facility has not evaluated operational impacts if fire suppression water is unavailable during prolonged disruption.
library_ofcs: Consider conducting a tabletop exercise to simulate the operational impacts of prolonged fire suppression water unavailability.
Develop a detailed continuity plan that outlines specific actions and decision points for maintaining operations when fire suppression water is disrupted.
Establish a liaison with local emergency services to coordinate response and recovery efforts in case of fire suppression water unavailability.
Identify and secure alternative water sources that can be used for fire suppression in case of primary supply failure.

injections.vulnerability_text: Onsite water pumping/boosting depends on commercial power without backup power, increasing disruption risk during outages.
library_ofcs: Explore the installation of solar-powered or other renewable energy sources to provide backup power for critical water pumping systems.

injections.vulnerability_text: Wastewater service connections enter/leave the site at the same geographic location, creating a potential single point of failure.
library_ofcs: Consider implementing a redundant wastewater service connection that enters the site from a different geographic location to mitigate shared entry risks.
Develop a contingency plan that includes alternative wastewater routing options in case of disruption at the shared entry point.
Install physical barriers and signage to protect the shared wastewater entry point from accidental vehicle impacts.

injections.vulnerability_text: Wastewater service components are collocated with other utilities, increasing the likelihood a single incident disrupts multiple services.
library_ofcs: Consider implementing a geographic separation strategy for wastewater service connections to reduce the risk of simultaneous disruption.
Evaluate and implement alternative routing for wastewater service connections to ensure redundancy in case of a single-point failure.
Develop a contingency plan that includes alternative wastewater service connections to mitigate disruptions caused by shared utility corridors.

injections.vulnerability_text: The facility has not identified its wastewater provider and/or primary upstream dependencies supporting service, which may limit effective coordination during disruptions.
library_ofcs: Consider engaging with local emergency management agencies to understand their protocols for wastewater service disruptions.

injections.vulnerability_text: Onsite wastewater pumping relies on commercial power without backup power, increasing the likelihood of overflow/backflow or operational disruption during outages.
library_ofcs: Consider installing a dedicated generator or uninterruptible power supply (UPS) specifically for the wastewater pumping system.

injections.vulnerability_text: The facility lacks temporary holding/containment capability to prevent overflow/backflow during wastewater service disruption, which may force shutdown.
library_ofcs: Consider integrating wastewater holding capacity into emergency response plans to ensure operational continuity during disruptions.
Consider evaluating the feasibility of on-site treatment options to manage wastewater during service disruptions.

injections.vulnerability_text: The facility has not evaluated regulatory/operational constraints that could force shutdown during prolonged wastewater disruption.
library_ofcs: Consider developing a detailed regulatory compliance plan that outlines actions to be taken during prolonged wastewater disruptions.
Consider establishing a contingency plan with the local utility provider to ensure priority restoration in case of extended service disruptions.
Consider training staff on the regulatory and operational constraints that could lead to facility shutdown during prolonged wastewater disruptions.
Consider integrating wastewater disruption scenarios into regular business continuity and disaster recovery drills.
